title: Real-Time PCR for Early Detection of Emerging Foodborne Threats
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Foodborne     pathogens     remain     a     major     public     health     threat  ,   causing     significant     illness     and     economic     losses     globally  .   Traditional     microbiological   testing   is     often     too     slow     to     support     timely     interventions  . Real-  time   PCR (  qPCR  )   offers     rapid  ,   sensitive  ,   and     specific     detection     of     pathogens  ,   enabling     early     response     and     reducing     outbreak     impact  . By   amplifying   target DNA   sequences   in real-  time  ,   qPCR     provides     results     within     hours     instead     of     days  , making   it     an     essential     tool     for     food     safety     programs  .   Its     application     allows     simultaneous   testing   for     multiple     pathogens  ,   supporting     surveillance     systems     and     supply     chain     monitoring  .   Implementing     qPCR   as   part     of     national     food     safety     strategies     will     strengthen     outbreak     preparedness  ,   enhance     consumer     protection  ,   and     build     public     trust   in   food     systems  .
